title: Megabonk
edition_pinned: Megabonk current Steam build as of 2026-07 (vedinad; Windows & Linux; first released 18 September 2025). Pins the 3D survivor-like loop documented on the live Steam page and early post-launch coverage: procedural maps, auto combat, random upgrades that vary in rarity, Silver meta, quests. Launch-day exact counts/mechanics are not preservable from mutable store/wiki material — later content updates may diverge.
moat: 3D vertical maps plus jump/slide speed turns survivor auto-combat into mobility routing, not only 2D kiting.

loop_skeleton.description: Navigate a procedurally generated 3D map with vertical routes, auto-attack hordes and bosses, collect XP gems for random upgrade offers, complete in-run quests and elites, die or survive, then spend scarce Silver for permanent unlocks.

depth_mechanisms.name: 3d vertical kite space
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Procedural third-person maps include height, ramps, and multi-level routes. Movement uses jump, slide, and speed boosts so positioning is a 3D pathing problem rather than planar orbiting alone.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If maps are flattened to 2D with no jump, slide, or height, route choice collapses to planar kiting and the game stops differentiating from flat survivor-likes on movement.

depth_mechanisms.name: random upgrade synergy offers
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Level-ups present random upgrade offers that vary in rarity across weapons and items so runs assemble different weapon-and-item synergies rather than a fixed unlock order. Selection is not claimed as rarity-weighted sampling.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If upgrade offers are fixed sequential unlocks with no random forks, run-to-run builds converge and second-session motive to try another synergy weakens.

depth_mechanisms.name: scarce silver meta
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Silver is uncommon meta currency earned from challenges, silvery-blue pots, and Silver Tome upgrades; it permanently unlocks characters, weapons, and tomes rather than flooding every run with permanent power. Quests gate roster/content separately and are not claimed as the primary Silver source.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If Silver drops at the same rate as common gold every run, challenge-gated unlock goals stop structuring second-session play.

depth_mechanisms.name: quest-gated roster unlocks
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: A large quest list (200+) gates characters, weapons, and other content, so meta progress is task-shaped rather than pure time-played. Exact per-character gate conditions vary by quest; not every roster entry is proven gated only by mid-round combat quests.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If all characters unlock by cumulative XP only, players stop hunting quest objectives as the path to new roster options.

feel_signature: A slide or jump commits a 3D path while auto-weapons fire; a level-up pause forces a random rarity-varying pick that changes how the next wave is cleared.
second_session_answer: Players return to earn scarce Silver (challenges, pots, Silver Tome) and complete quests that gate the next character or tome, then test a new mobility-and-weapon synergy on procedural maps.
packaging_lessons: Inference: ship auto-combat first, then add 3D movement (jump/slide/speed) as the differentiator from 2D survivor-likes.
Inference: keep meta currency scarce (challenge/pot/tome-linked) and quests task-shaped so unlocks remain session goals after the first clear.
anti_lessons: Do not treat Megabonk as a skin of Vampire Survivors — copying auto-attack without 3D vertical mobility misses the moat.
Do not flood Silver; scarcity is load-bearing for quest-driven return.

system_bom.note: 3D move + auto-attack + XP pick is the run core that either ends in death or longer survival
items, chests, and upgrade offers change combat options mid-run so the next wave is cleared differently
gems from kills drive level-up offers that fork the build
random upgrades that vary in rarity are the decision points that assemble run synergies
Silver unlocks characters, weapons, and tomes across runs so permanent progress sits outside a single survival
quest list gates roster and content so return goals are task-shaped
Steam stats/leaderboards support score-attack return after unlock goals settle
death ends the run; survival length and bosses structure success

citations.claim: Edition pin: first released 18 September 2025 for Windows and Linux by vedinad as a 3D survivor-like with procedural maps, auto combat, and random upgrades; card pins current Steam build as of 2026-07 because launch-state evidence is not preservable from mutable store/wiki material.
Edition pin caveat: later content updates (e.g. December mega-update coverage) are out of pin; mechanics rest on current Steam description plus early post-launch docs, not a frozen launch dump.
3d vertical kite space: third-person maps with verticality; slide, jump, speed boosts; multi-directional movement.
Random upgrade synergy offers: each upgrade offer is random and will vary in rarity (Steam); not claimed as rarity-weighted selection.
Scarce silver meta: Silver via challenges, pots, Silver Tome; permanent unlocks; not common. Quests not claimed as Silver source.
Quest-gated roster surface: 20 characters, 70+ items, 200+ quests documented.
Quest list details for content gating.
Character roster count and unlock surface (20 characters).
Feel signature: 3D movement with auto-combat and level-up upgrade picks during horde survival.
Feel signature / loop comparison as 3D survivor-like with Risk of Rain 2 × Vampire Survivors framing (early coverage).
Second-session answer: scarce Silver (challenges, pots, Silver Tome) and permanent unlocks structure return; quests are a separate content-gating surface.
Play-loop BOM: procedural 3D map, auto attack, XP upgrades is the run atom.
Loot BOM: items and upgrade offers change combat mid-run.
XP and leveling BOM: gems and random upgrades that vary in rarity are decision points.
Meta-progression BOM: Silver unlocks permanent content across runs.
Quests BOM: 200+ quests gate roster and content.
Leaderboards BOM: Steam-facing competitive stats support score-attack return (store feature surface).
Win-loss-death BOM: death ends the run; survival and bosses structure success.
Packaging lesson 1 (labeled Inference): auto-combat base plus 3D movement differentiator.
Packaging lesson 2 (labeled Inference): scarce Silver and quest-gated roster keep unlock goals after first clear; quests are not claimed as the Silver source.
Anti-lesson VS skin: 3D terrain and verticality are the cited differentiator.
Anti-lesson flood Silver: scarcity is load-bearing for challenge-gated unlocks.
